faq.question: Is group strength good for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. All workouts are coach-led and scalable to any fitness level.

faq.question: Do I need to be in shape before starting?
faq.answer: No. We meet you where you are and scale everything.

faq.question: Do I have to sign a long-term contract?
faq.answer: No. We offer flexible month-to-month memberships.

faq.question: How long are classes?
faq.answer: Classes are 60 minutes: warm-up, strength or skill work, workout, and cool down.

faq.question: What should I bring to my first workout?
faq.answer: Water, comfortable training shoes, and clothes you can move in.

faq.question: Do you help with nutrition?
faq.answer: Yes. Nutrition coaching is available with every membership.

hero.headline: Built around people, not machines
hero.subheading: Since 2018, Ironwood Strength has helped Torrance neighbors build strength and confidence side by side.

about.gymStory: Ironwood Strength opened in 2018 when two Torrance coaches noticed that big-box gyms were leaving everyday athletes behind. They wanted a place where beginners felt welcome and experienced members still had room to grow.

story.headline: How Ironwood Strength started
story.subheadline: Two coaches, one empty warehouse, and a belief that fitness should be accessible.
story.imageAlt: The original Ironwood Strength space in Torrance

blocks.html: Marcus Chen and Dana Ortiz met while coaching at a commercial gym in Torrance. They kept watching new members sign up, wander the floor for a few weeks, and quietly stop showing up. The equipment was there, but the guidance wasn't.

In 2018 they leased a small warehouse off Hawthorne Boulevard and built Ironwood Strength around one idea: coaching first. No sales pressure, no cardio theater, just structured strength and conditioning with a coach in every class.

What started as a handful of evening classes grew into a full gym community. Today Ironwood trains busy adults from Torrance, Redondo Beach, and Palos Verdes, focusing on long-term strength, conditioning, and health habits that stick.

about.communityHeadline: A community that keeps you showing up
about.communityBody:
At Ironwood Strength, the workout is only part of the draw. The room is filled with teachers, parents, nurses, and engineers who all have the same goal: show up, do the work, and get a little better than yesterday. Coaches know your name, ask about your injuries, and adjust the plan when life gets busy.

We program in 4-week blocks so you can see progress without guessing. And when you miss a week, someone texts to check in. That accountability is why our members stay for years, not months.
about.ctaHeadline: Come see what makes Ironwood different

team.name: Marcus Chen
team.title: Co-founder & Head Coach
team.bio: CSCS-certified. Former collegiate strength coach. Specializes in beginner barbell technique and post-rehab return to training.

team.name: Dana Ortiz
team.title: Co-founder & Coach
team.bio: NASM-CPT with 10 years in South Bay fitness. Runs Ironwood's nutrition and lifestyle coaching program.

team.name: Sam Park
team.title: Strength Coach
team.bio: USA Weightlifting L1 coach. Known for detailed warm-ups and making the barbell feel approachable.

team.name: Riley Brooks
team.title: Conditioning Coach
team.bio: Former track athlete who builds conditioning workouts that don't destroy your knees.

faq.question: Who is Ironwood Strength best for?
faq.answer: Busy adults in Torrance and the South Bay who want structured coaching, scalable workouts, and a supportive community.

faq.question: Do I need to be fit before I start?
faq.answer: No. Every class is coach-led and scaled to your current ability. Beginners are the majority of our new members.

faq.question: What makes Ironwood different from a regular gym?
faq.answer: Coach-led classes, 4-week programming blocks, and a community that holds you accountable. You don't figure it out alone.

faq.question: Where is Ironwood Strength located?
faq.answer: We're at 24444 Hawthorne Blvd in Torrance, CA 90505, easy to reach from Redondo Beach and Palos Verdes.

faq.question: Can I try a class before joining?
faq.answer: Yes. Your first intro session is free. Book through the contact page and we'll pair you with a coach.

faq.question: Do you offer personal training?
faq.answer: Yes. We offer 1-on-1 training plus nutrition and lifestyle coaching for members who want a fully customized plan.
